author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
"How dare we use something like that on someone's dream?" – Why generative AI artwork is a no-go for The Scroll of Taiwu

Long before the global success of Genshin Impact and Black Myth: Wukong, The Scroll of Taiwu from ConchShip Games pointed towards the potentially huge market for Chinese-developed titles.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Thatgamecompany's 20-year journey from anti-Columbine school project to a celebration of Van Gogh

Jenova Chen, the co-founder of Thatgamecompany, never seriously considered making video games when he was younger. "Where I grew up, video games were seen as gambling, and oversexualized kind of soft porn, and overviolenced. Nobody respected video games. My parents would be ashamed if I told them I would be making video games."

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Auditors warn Don't Nod will run out of cash by November if it is unable to secure financing

French game developer Don't Nod could run out of money by November if it is unable to secure further financing.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
TIGA appoints nine industry veterans to board of directors

UK video games trade body TIGA has added nine new members to its board of directors.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Research suggests exercise games like Ring Fit Adventure could treat early stages of depression

New research shows that exercise games – or 'exergames' – like Nintendo's Ring Fit Adventure could help people deal with the early stages of depression.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
President of Germany to open Gamescom Congress and attend Gamescom 2026

Federal President of Germany Frank-Walter Steinmeier will attend Gamescom and open Gamescom Congress, the event's summit on the intersection of games and culture, politics and technology. Steinmeier will be the first German head of state to attend gamescom.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Kwalee Labs shut down following launch of Luna Abyss

Kwalee Labs, the developer behind the well-received PC and PS5 shooter Luna Abyss, has been shuttered only a month after the game launched. The firm was originally founded as Bonsai Collective, which Kwalee purchased out of administration in September, according to a LinkedIn post by former creative director Benni Hill.

Read more
